revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:異形鉄器]]
[[File:異形鉄器.png|alt=Model diagram of Igyō TekkiAll based on the measured drawings of Hashimoto & Fujii 2007, pp. 40, 57–58|thumb]] '''Igyō Tekki''' (異形鉄器) is a type of iron tool ({{ill|Ironware|ja|鉄器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ironware|1=iron tool|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11649539}}) excavated from archaeological sites dating to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun Period|1=Kofun period}} in the southern part of the {{ill|Kyushu region|ja|九州地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州|1=Kyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13987}}. It is considered to be a kind of weapon corresponding to an {{ill|iron arrowhead|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowhead|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, or to a sword ({{ill|Ken|ja|剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken|1=Double-edged sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396047}}) or spear ({{ill|Spear|en|Spear|qid=Q44475|lt=Yari|1=Spear}}). The specimens from the {{ill|Naramikawa Site|ja|成川遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramikawa Site|1=Narikawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q38267026}} in Yamakawa Naramikawa, {{ill|Ibusuki|en|Ibusuki|qid=Q19712|lt=Ibusuki City|1=Ibusuki}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} are particularly well known.
== Overview == The term “Igyō Tekki” is an extremely vague one. It refers to ironware with a blade component (such as arrowheads or swords) that, although similar in that respect, displays a markedly different shape from other ironware of the same period, making it difficult at first glance to determine its intended use or function. Since early finds – especially as described in the excavation report of the Naramikawa Site ({{Sfn|文化庁|1974}}) – were characterized as “異形” (“strange form”), this term has continued to be used.
=== Morphology === Igyō Tekki are characterized by having a double-edged blade, with the portion corresponding to the hilt’s connection (the “nakago” [{{ill|Stem (of a sword)|ja|茎 (刀)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stem (of a sword)|1=nakago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1963918}}]) splitting into two branches (legs). Near the base of these legs, two to four small oval or circular holes are frequently present. In many cases, a rod-like wooden element is preserved between these holes, leading scholars to believe that the hilt was attached by a “ne-gakami” method – that is, by clamping the ironware from both sides with a vertically split wooden stick passed through the holes and secured with a string. These examples indicate that many Igyō Tekki represent an enlarged form of the {{ill|iron arrowhead|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowhead|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}.
They are broadly classified into two types – the sword type and the arrowhead type – based on a total length of approximately 17–18 cm{{Sfn|Hashimoto|Fujii|2007|p=37}}{{Sfn|Wada|2017|pp=43-44}}.
==== Arrowhead Type ==== This type features a double-edged blade with legs that open in a “ha” shape (as seen, for example, in the case of the Machida-hori underground horizontal tomb group) or in a “gani-mata” (crab-like) shape (as seen in the Shimohori No.2 underground horizontal tomb). Alternatively, as in some examples from the Naramikawa Site, the artifact may lack clearly defined legs and instead exhibit an overall shape resembling a leaf or a shogi piece, with a slight notched concavity at the bottom. Additionally, one example from the Naramikawa Site is considered to be an arrowhead with a stem and a lacy, triangular pattern.
==== Sword Type ==== This type is found only at the Naramikawa Site, with five examples excavated. In this type, the long, double-edged blade is accompanied by legs that either extend somewhat inward from the base toward the tip, or by legs that widen only at the very end. Due to its size, it is considered either a sword or a spear, though it may also be an extremely enlarged and stylized iron arrowhead.
== Distribution == As of 2017, only 17 examples from 5 archaeological sites have been reported{{Sfn|Wada|2017|p=45}}, and they are exclusively concentrated in southern Kyushu – from the southern part of {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} to the Osumi Peninsula of {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, and along the southern tip of the {{ill|Satsuma Peninsula|en|Satsuma Peninsula|qid=Q1322067|lt=Satsuma Peninsula|1=Satsuma Peninsula}}. Notably, they are frequently found as funerary offerings (副葬品) associated with the uniquely regional {{ill|underground horizontal tomb|ja|地下式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/underground horizontal tomb|1=Underground Horizontal Yokoanabo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11424056}} groups. At the Naramikawa Site, Igyō Tekki are scattered throughout the tomb area (the group of {{ill|Dozukehai Tombs|ja|土壙墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dozukehai Tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土坑墓|1=Pit graves|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10856121}}) alongside other iron weapons such as the {{ill|Jakyōken|ja|蛇行剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakyōken|1=Dakōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11625537}}, suggesting that they were not placed in individual tombs but rather contributed collectively to the tomb complex as shared burial relics{{Sfn|Hashimoto|Fujii|2007|p=55}}.
Furthermore, although a formal excavation report has not been published, several studies{{Sfn|Niwayashiki|2015|p=1-6}}{{Sfn|Wada|2017|p=42}} mention that additional examples have been found at the Tachino-hori Site in {{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|qid=Q819578|lt=Kanoya City|1=Kanoya, Kagoshima}}, Kushira-chō, further attesting to the restricted geographic distribution.
== Archaeological Sites with Igyō Tekki == === Kagoshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Naramikawa Site|ja|成川遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramikawa Site|1=Narikawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q38267026}} (in {{ill|Ibusuki|en|Ibusuki|qid=Q19712|lt=Ibusuki City|1=Ibusuki}}, Yamakawa Naramikawa): 7 examples (5 sword-type, 2 arrowhead-type) * Tomb No.2 of the Shimohori Underground Horizontal Tomb Group (in {{ill|Soo District, Kagoshima|en|Soo District, Kagoshima|qid=Q1043870|lt=So-oku District|1=Soo District, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|qid=Q931439|lt=Osaki Town|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}, Okabe): 1 example (arrowhead-type) * Machida-hori Underground Horizontal Tomb Group (in {{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|qid=Q819578|lt=Kanoya City|1=Kanoya, Kagoshima}}, Kushira-chō, Hosayamada): 6 examples (arrowhead-type)
=== Miyazaki Prefecture === * Matsunomoto Underground Horizontal Tomb (in {{ill|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|en|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|qid=Q861283|lt=Kobayashi City|1=Kobayashi, Miyazaki}}, Ōaza Tsutsumi): 1 example (arrowhead-type) * Daikubo Site No.1 (in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|qid=Q638301|lt=Tosu City|1=Miyakonojō}}, Takajo Town, Arimizu): 2 examples (arrowhead-type)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Underground Horizontal Yokoanabo|ja|地下式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Horizontal Yokoanabo|qq=}} * {{ill|Tateishi Doko Bo|ja|立石土壙墓|1=Standing Pit Tomb|2=ja|3=立石土壙墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateishi Doko Bo|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Pit Tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|Dakōken|ja|蛇行剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dakōken|qq=}} * {{ill|Narikawa-style Pottery|ja|成川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narikawa-style Pottery|1=Narikawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q38276609}}
* {{ill|Tsukushi script|ja|筑紫文字|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi script|qq=}}
* {{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=文化庁|title=Naramikawa Site, Located in Yamakawa Town, Ibusuki District, Kagoshima Prefecture – Excavation Report of Cultural Properties, Volume 7|date=1974-02|year=1974|publisher=吉川弘文館|ncid=BN09420474|ref={{SfnRef|文化庁|1974}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Hashimoto|first=Tatsuya|authorlink=橋本達也 (考古学者)|last2=Fujii|first2=Daisuke|title=Research on Kofun Period Funerary Customs Other Than Tombs|publisher={{ill|Kagoshima University Museum|en|Kagoshima University Museum|qid=Q48746463|lt=Kagoshima University Comprehensive Research Museum|1=Kagoshima University Museum}}|year=2007|date=2007-03|ncid=BA81981477|ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Niwayashiki|first=Kumiko|year=2015|date=2015-10|title=A Brief Consideration on Ironware Excavated from Underground Horizontal Tombs in the Kofun Period Plains|url=http://www.jomon-no-mori.jp/jimages/mbn_kiyo/kiyo8/kiyo8_1-6.pdf|journal=研究紀要・年報 縄文の森から|volume=8|pages=1-6|publisher={{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Archaeological Center|ja|鹿児島県立埋蔵文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Archaeological Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|ref=harv}}{{リンク切れ|date=2024th year 5月}} * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Wada|first=Rikei|year=2017|date=2017-04|title=Short Study on Igyō Tekki – On the Unknown Ironware Excavated from Daikubo Site No.1|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/maibun/wp/wp-content/uploads/2017/04/kiyou-04.pdf|journal=宮崎県埋蔵文化財センター研究紀要|volume=4|pages=42-46|publisher={{ill|Miyazaki Prefectural Archaeological Center|ja|宮崎県埋蔵文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Prefectural Archaeological Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|ref=harv}}{{リンク切れ|date=2024th year 5月}}
